# HPD.ML.BinaryClassification

Generated from published HPD ML `0.5.0` packages.

## HPD-ML-BinaryClassification

- [`AveragedPerceptronLearner`](../types/hpd-ml-binaryclassification-averagedperceptronlearner.md) - Online averaged perceptron. Updates weights when a prediction is wrong, averages weights across all iterations. The averaged weights generalize better than the final weights (voted perceptron theory). Supports online learning via InitialModel — resume training on new data.
- [`AveragedPerceptronOptions`](../types/hpd-ml-binaryclassification-averagedperceptronoptions.md) - Undocumented in the published XML file.
- [`BinaryClassificationExtensions`](../types/hpd-ml-binaryclassification-binaryclassificationextensions.md) - C# 14 extension members for binary classification learner discovery.
- [`CalibratorTransform`](../types/hpd-ml-binaryclassification-calibratortransform.md) - Platt scaling: learns sigmoid parameters A, B to map raw scores to calibrated probabilities. P(y=1|score) = 1 / (1 + exp(A*score + B)) Used by SVM and Perceptron which don't produce calibrated outputs natively.
- [`LinearModelParameters`](../types/hpd-ml-binaryclassification-linearmodelparameters.md) - Learned parameters for any linear binary classifier. Weights + bias, optionally with per-feature statistics.
- [`LinearScoringTransform`](../types/hpd-ml-binaryclassification-linearscoringtransform.md) - Scores rows using w·x + b, then applies sigmoid for probability. Adds "Score" (raw), "Probability" (sigmoid), and "PredictedLabel" columns.
- [`LinearSvmLearner`](../types/hpd-ml-binaryclassification-linearsvmlearner.md) - Linear SVM via PEGASOS (Primal Estimated sub-GrAdient SOlver for SVM). Minimizes: (λ/2)||w||² + (1/n)Σmax(0, 1 - y(w·x + b)) PEGASOS alternates between SGD steps on the hinge loss and projection to the L2 ball of radius 1/√λ.
- [`LinearSvmOptions`](../types/hpd-ml-binaryclassification-linearsvmoptions.md) - Undocumented in the published XML file.
- [`LogisticRegressionLearner`](../types/hpd-ml-binaryclassification-logisticregressionlearner.md) - Logistic regression via L-BFGS optimization. Loss function: -Σ[y·log(σ(w·x+b)) + (1-y)·log(1-σ(w·x+b))] / n L-BFGS uses analytic gradients and gradient history to approximate the inverse Hessian.
- [`LogisticRegressionOptions`](../types/hpd-ml-binaryclassification-logisticregressionoptions.md) - Undocumented in the published XML file.
- [`SdcaLearner`](../types/hpd-ml-binaryclassification-sdcalearner.md) - Stochastic Dual Coordinate Ascent for binary classification. SDCA works on the dual formulation of the regularized loss. For logistic loss: updates dual variables α_i and primal weights w simultaneously. Unlike L-BFGS, SDCA doesn't need to hold the full gradient in memory — it updates one dual variable at a time, making it efficient for large datasets.
- [`SdcaOptions`](../types/hpd-ml-binaryclassification-sdcaoptions.md) - Undocumented in the published XML file.
- [`WeightStatistics`](../types/hpd-ml-binaryclassification-weightstatistics.md) - Undocumented in the published XML file.
